verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Rey Cortiço Mid-morning — 11:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço looks like the best bodyboard option for mid-morning — 11:00.  This should feel like a fun progression session rather than a heavy one.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 65 kJ, 9 s period, lully 44/100, and glassy conditions.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: scan from the dunes first because banks and channels shift fast.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean fun-sized window for Heavy / advanced, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 52 kJ, 9 s period, lully 41/100, and glassy conditions.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort.  Watch: 6-9s at mid tide stays weak and soft for Supertubos / Molhe Leste; Supertubos is a check-first bodyboard size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is helping, but the spot still looks serious.

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 54 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro may feel too soft without more push

ranked.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 152 kJ, 9 s period, consistent 73/100, and glassy conditions.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at mid tide is still too weak in the sheltered bay.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; 6-9s at mid tide is still too weak in the sheltered bay; Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
warnings: 6-9s at mid tide is still too weak in the sheltered bay
Cantinho da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size
